content.mi:
Te Akoranga Kore Kupu

I te hokinga mai o Te Tahi, i reira tonu tōna iwi.

I titiro ngā tāne ki a ia me ngā kanohi whānui, kāore he kupu, engari kua kī ngā ngākau i te whakamā.

Kāore a Te Tahi i riri.

Kāore ia i whiua.

Ka kī noa ia:

“Waiho mā te whakamā e patu.”

Let shame be the punishment.

Ā, ka mārama te iwi.
content.en:
The Unspoken Lesson

When Te Tahi returned home, his people were there.

The men looked at him with wide eyes, their mouths silent, their hearts shamed.

Te Tahi did not shout.

He did not punish.

He simply said:

“Waiho mā te whakamā e patu.”

Let shame be the punishment.

And the people understood.
